Brand: Clover Hill Dairy (relabeled as KESSO, QUESOS LA RICURA, IZALCO, DE MI PUEBLO, RIO LINDO)
Product: EXPANDED RECALL ‚Äî all Clover Hill Dairy brand cheese (hard and soft). Includes: Soft Cuajada in Brine (5-gal bucket 30-lb, 2-gal bucket 14-lb); Soft Cuajada vacuum sealed (5-lb bags, 2.5-lb clamshell, 0.90-lb / 14-oz clamshell); Cuajada vacuum sealed (30-lb boxes); Ricotta (5-gal bucket 40-lb, 2-gal bucket 18-lb, 2.5-lb tubs, 1-lb clamshell); Soft Cuajada Crumbs; Mild Cheese Varieties ‚Äî Yummy Cheddar, White Cheddar, White Colby, Monterey Jack, Marble Jack (¬Ω-lb, 1-lb, 2.5-lb, 5-lb, 40-lb); Sharp Cheese Varieties ‚Äî Cheddar, Yummy Cheddar, White Cheddar, Monterey Jack; Flavored ‚Äî Horseradish, Old Bay Cheddar; Pepper Jack Varieties ‚Äî Jalape√±o Cheddar, Pepper Jack, Sizzlin Colby with Habanero Peppers; Smoked Cheddar (Cheddar, White Cheddar); Smoked Pepper ‚Äî Jalape√±o Cheddar; plus Fresh Cheddar Curd and assorted Snack Pack / 3-in-a-Pack stix formats. Plant permit number 24-128 printed on clamshell containers.
Reason: FDA Company Announcement + Publish Date both 2026-06-18. EXPANSION of the earlier Clover Hill Dairy recall (R57, 2026-06-09) which covered only Soft Ricotta/Reques√≥n cheese. The expansion broadens scope to ALL Clover Hill Dairy brand cheese due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. As of 2026-06-18, the outbreak count linked to soft cheese products stands at 9 illnesses, 8 hospitalizations, and 1 death. Distribution: direct retail market, farmers markets, and third-party distributors across NC, NY, VA, MD, NJ, and Washington DC. Products may be relabeled under brands KESSO, QUESOS LA RICURA, IZALCO, DE MI PUEBLO, and RIO LINDO. Consumers urged to check manufacturer permit number 24-128 on clamshell containers.
Notes: Manual add 2026-06-18. EXPANSION of Clover Hill Dairy Listeria recall cluster (original recall R57 2026-06-09 covered Ricotta/Reques√≥n only; CDC outbreak pages already at R91 investigation + R93 index dated 2026-06-04). All-brand expansion is a distinct regulatory action ‚Äî kept separately per AFTS multi-document cluster convention. Death toll increased from 0 to 1 since original recall. Linked to FDA Outbreak Advisory at fda.gov/food/outbreaks-foodborne-illness/outbreak-investigation-listeria-monocytogenes-soft-cheese-june-2026.

Product: Whole Milk Powdered Infant Formula ‚Äî Nara Organics. TWO SKUs: (1) 700 g can, UPC 860013251901; (2) 400 g can, UPC 860013251918. Sixteen lot codes affected (all lots currently on market): 408125075E14F2; 708125076E14F2; 708125083E14F2; 408125139E14F2; 708125141E14F2; 708125145E14F2; 708125174E14F2; 709125273E14F2; 709125280E14F2; 709125288E14F2; 70926019ENNB; 70926029ENNB; 70926035ENNB; 70926039ENNB; 70926042ENNB; 409125307E14F2. Three specific lots tied to confirmed infant illnesses: 709125280E14F2, 709125288E14F2, 708125174E14F2.
Reason: Voluntary recall (Company Announcement + FDA Publish Date both 2026-06-13). Nara Organics of New York, NY, voluntarily recalling all lots of Nara Organics Powdered Infant Formula "out of an abundance of caution due to the potential risk of Clostridium botulinum contamination". FDA and CDC contacted Nara Organics on 2026-06-12 with information about 3 cases of infant botulism. As of FDA publication, Nara infant formula has not tested positive for C. botulinum ‚Äî voluntary recall pending CDC/FDA root-cause investigation. Distribution: nationally via Target retail stores, Target.com, and Nara.com from July 2025 through June 2026. NOT distributed outside the USA.
Notes: PRIMARY SOURCE FIRM of the Nara Organics infant botulism outbreak. Cross-references: CDC outbreak investigation at /botulism/outbreaks-investigations/infant-formula-june-2026/investigation.html (existing CDC row); CDC outbreak index at /index.html (existing CDC row); FDA Outbreak Advisory at fda.gov/food/outbreaks-foodborne-illness/outbreak-investigation-infant-botulism-powdered-infant-formula-june-2026. Outbreak count: 3 confirmed infant botulism cases in California, Washington, Pennsylvania, all hospitalized + treated with BabyBIG (Botulism Immune Globulin Intravenous). No deaths reported. Promoted 2026-06-15 from Pending P22 (collector picked up via Google News supplement; the FDA URL resolver landed it correctly via the Nara Organics fda.gov page). fda_listing.py title-only filter again ‚Äî slug "possible-health-risk" without pathogen keyword (eighth confirmed instance of this scraper gap).
